stem: A urban planning study measures the commute time (minutes) for a sample of 50 city workers. The sample mean is 38.1 min with a sample standard deviation of 8.7 min. Assuming the data are approximately normally distributed, estimate the true population mean and provide a 95% confidence interval.

ground_truth.answer: Population mean ≈ 38.1 min. 95% CI = [35.6, 40.6]. Point estimate: 38.1 min.

common_failure_modes: Using z-critical value instead of t-critical for small samples
Not reporting the confidence interval alongside the point estimate
